paper_metadata.title: Text Embeddings by Weakly-Supervised Contrastive Pre-training
paper_metadata.abstract: This paper presents E5, a family of state-of-the-art text embeddings that transfer well to a wide range of tasks. The model is trained in a contrastive manner with weak supervision signals from our curated large-scale text pair dataset (called CCPairs). E5 can be readily used as a general-purpose embedding model for any tasks requiring a single-vector representation of texts such as retrieval, clustering, and classification, achieving strong performance in both zero-shot and fine-tuned settings. We conduct extensive evaluations on 56 datasets from the BEIR and MTEB benchmarks. For zero-shot settings, E5 is the first model that outperforms the strong BM25 baseline on the BEIR retrieval benchmark without using any labeled data. When fine-tuned, E5 obtains the best results on the MTEB benchmark, beating existing embedding models with 40x more parameters.

extraction_data.innovation: E5's primary innovation is weakly-supervised contrastive pre-training, which uses a curated text pair dataset (CCPairs) to learn high-quality text embeddings without labeled data for pre-training, enabling strong performance in both zero-shot and fine-tuned settings.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: CCPairs (curated from CommunityQA, CommonCrawl, and Scientific papers)
extraction_data.research_problem: Text Embedding for Information Retrieval and Text Matching
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning for retrieval, semantic textual similarity, classification, and summarization
